reforms.description: The Norwegian government established a Research and Innovation Fund with a capital of NOK 3 billion to increase R&D expenditures, aiming to reach the OECD average for total R&D expenditures within five years. Additionally, a White Paper on research and development was published, setting the same goal of reaching the OECD average for total R&D expenditures in five years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D expenditures is expected to enhance innovation and productivity, aligning with OECD standards.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund was established and operationalized in 1999.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a significant fund and the publication of a strategic White Paper represent major structural changes in R&D funding.
reforms.source_quote: The government published a White Paper on research and development, setting the goal of reaching the OECD average for total R&D expenditures in five years.

reforms.description: The government plans to change the funding model for universities to base budgets more on research activity rather than student numbers, promoting a stronger focus on research output.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Aligning funding with research activity is expected to incentivize higher research output and quality.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding priorities for universities.
reforms.source_quote: The government intends to change the funding of universities, with budgets based to a greater degree on research activity.

reforms.description: The government announced an evaluation of the Research Council of Norway, which was created to fund research, indicating a potential reform in how research funding is managed and allocated.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of the evaluation on future funding practices is uncertain at this stage.
reforms.status_evidence: The evaluation has been announced but not yet conducted.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the evaluation may lead to significant changes, it is currently an assessment rather than a reform.
reforms.source_quote: In its White Paper, the government announced an evaluation of the Research Council of Norway, created in 1993 to fund research.
